background.text:
1. Background
On September 7, 2018, an Authority to Construct was issued for the installation of the below equipment located 
at Tesoro Refining and Manufacturing Company LLC (Tesoro) : 
S-1517  Coker Flare  
Abated by  
A-1583  Flare Gas H 2S Treatment System, Caustic Scrubbers (2)  & Knockout Drum , VaporLock  
This addendum serves to capture subsequent  changes to the conditions  after issuance  of the Authority to 
Construct. These revisions were made  in response to Tesoro’s comments concerning the monitoring of sulfur 
remov ed by A -1583.  
For reference, Tesoro’s comments are summarized below: 
• Concerning Part 69g –  This condition is unnecessary as there is no specific target for how much the H 2S 
treatment is required to remove.  
• Concerning Part 70 –  This condition is the same a s Part 69 and is redundant with respect to Condition 8077 
requirements.  
 
As noted in the engineering eval uation, section 5, t hese comments were consider ed by the District, however, 
upon  initial assessment , they were  deemed unwarranted without additional  information concerning  Tesoro ’s 
fulfillment of the  regulatory sulfur recovery requirements stated in  Regulation 9 -1-313.2.  Therefore, the District 
did not implement any revisions. D ue to the tight timeline in which Tesoro needed to install  A-1583  before the 
turnaround, Tesoro accepted issuance of the Authority to Construct with Condition 23129 “as -is”, contingent 
upon the further review and amendment of the referenced per mit conditions by the District before issuance of 
the final permit to operat e. 
The District requested that Tesoro submit additional information concerning the design  parameters  of A-1583. 
In response, Tesoro submitted information showing that , subject to a fixed inlet concentration of H 2S, greater 
than 95%  sulfur removal can be ac hieved by A -1583 (see Attachment A).  The following section summarizes the 
District’s review of this information and subsequent revisions  to the referenced condition parts.  
2. District’s  Review & Revision   
Part 69 g.  
As seen from the  information provided by Tesoro,  A-1583 is designed to output  a stream with a maximum H2S 
concentration of 80 ppmv. This design allows  A-1583 to achieve  95% sulfur recovery  only if the inlet  
concentr ation of H 2S remains above 3240 ppmv . Therefore , to ensure a sulfur recovery  of at least 95 %, the 
District would have to limit the inlet H 2S concentration to above  a certain amount. This type of limitation could 
be detrimental to the  overall goal of sulfur reduction.  
In addition, as stated in Regulation 9 -1-313. 2, the 95% recovery applies on a refinery-wide basis and is not 
meant to apply to a single piece of equipment. For the reasons stated, the District has amended the conditions 
to allow for either measurement of sulfur recovered by A-1583 or total exclusion of the sulfur recovered by       
2 
 A-1583 when performing a refinery -wide balance to determine sulfur recovery . This is summarized in the revised 
part 69 g below:  
69 g . The owner/operator shall choose one of the following recordkeeping/evaluation methods for determination of 
sulfur recovery on a refinery -wide basis during the operation of A -1583:  
i.  Record amount of sulfur removed from the vent gas in A -1583 OR 
ii. Exclude amount of sulfur removed from the vent gas in A -1583 from  the calculation of total sulfur 
recovered on a refinery- wide basis.   
 
Part 70 
To address Tesoro’s issue of redundancy from the implementation of both Condition 8077 and Condition 23129, 
Part 70, this condition  part was revised to  reference 8077 only if t he operation of A-1583 is being u sed in 
replacement of a measure defined under 8077 -B9C (i -vi). These revisions  are summarized  as follows:  
70. If the owner/operator is using the operation of A- 1583 in lieu of any measure set forth per 
Condition 8077 -B9C (i -vi) then the owner/operator shall include a summary of the A- 1583 operation 
during the turnaround, including vent gas flow, H2S concentrations, and refinery -wide sulfur 
recovery, in the written follow -up report required by Condition 8077 -B9C(viii).  
(Basis: Recordkeeping, NSPS, Regulation 9- 1-313.2, Regulation 12- 11-401)  
 
The complete condition that will be issued as a part of the  permit to oper ate is stated in the following section.

blocks.heading: EVALUATION REPORT
blocks.text:
EVALUATION REPORT
Tesoro Refining and Manufacturing Company LLC  
150 Solano  Way , Martinez, CA 94553  
Plant # 14628, Application # 29416 
1. Background  
On September 7, 2018, an Authority to Construct was issued for the installation of the below equipment located 
at Tesoro Refining and Manufacturing Company LLC (Tesoro) : 
S-1517  Coker Flare  
Abated by  
A-1583  Flare Gas H 2S Treatment System, Caustic Scrubbers (2)  & Knockout Drum , VaporLock  
This addendum serves to capture subsequent  changes to the conditions  after issuance  of the Authority to 
Construct. These revisions were made  in response to Tesoro’s comments concerning the monitoring of sulfur 
remov ed by A -1583.  
For reference, Tesoro’s comments are summarized below: 
• Concerning Part 69g –  This condition is unnecessary as there is no specific target for how much the H 2S 
treatment is required to remove.  
• Concerning Part 70 –  This condition is the same a s Part 69 and is redundant with respect to Condition 8077 
requirements.  
 
As noted in the engineering eval uation, section 5, t hese comments were consider ed by the District, however, 
upon  initial assessment , they were  deemed unwarranted without additional  information concerning  Tesoro ’s 
fulfillment of the  regulatory sulfur recovery requirements stated in  Regulation 9 -1-313.2.  Therefore, the District 
did not implement any revisions. D ue to the tight timeline in which Tesoro needed to install  A-1583  before the 
turnaround, Tesoro accepted issuance of the Authority to Construct with Condition 23129 “as -is”, contingent 
upon the further review and amendment of the referenced per mit conditions by the District before issuance of 
the final permit to operat e. 
The District requested that Tesoro submit additional information concerning the design  parameters  of A-1583. 
In response, Tesoro submitted information showing that , subject to a fixed inlet concentration of H 2S, greater 
than 95%  sulfur removal can be ac hieved by A -1583 (see Attachment A).  The following section summarizes the 
District’s review of this information and subsequent revisions  to the referenced condition parts.  
2. District’s  Review & Revision   
Part 69 g.  
As seen from the  information provided by Tesoro,  A-1583 is designed to output  a stream with a maximum H2S 
concentration of 80 ppmv. This design allows  A-1583 to achieve  95% sulfur recovery  only if the inlet  
concentr ation of H 2S remains above 3240 ppmv . Therefore , to ensure a sulfur recovery  of at least 95 %, the 
District would have to limit the inlet H 2S concentration to above  a certain amount. This type of limitation could 
be detrimental to the  overall goal of sulfur reduction.  
In addition, as stated in Regulation 9 -1-313. 2, the 95% recovery applies on a refinery-wide basis and is not 
meant to apply to a single piece of equipment. For the reasons stated, the District has amended the conditions 
to allow for either measurement of sulfur recovered by A-1583 or total exclusion of the sulfur recovered by       
2 
 A-1583 when performing a refinery -wide balance to determine sulfur recovery . This is summarized in the revised 
part 69 g below:  
69 g . The owner/operator shall choose one of the following recordkeeping/evaluation methods for determination of 
sulfur recovery on a refinery -wide basis during the operation of A -1583:  
i.  Record amount of sulfur removed from the vent gas in A -1583 OR 
ii. Exclude amount of sulfur removed from the vent gas in A -1583 from  the calculation of total sulfur 
recovered on a refinery- wide basis.   
 
Part 70 
To address Tesoro’s issue of redundancy from the implementation of both Condition 8077 and Condition 23129, 
Part 70, this condition  part was revised to  reference 8077 only if t he operation of A-1583 is being u sed in 
replacement of a measure defined under 8077 -B9C (i -vi). These revisions  are summarized  as follows:  
70. If the owner/operator is using the operation of A- 1583 in lieu of any measure set forth per 
Condition 8077 -B9C (i -vi) then the owner/operator shall include a summary of the A- 1583 operation 
during the turnaround, including vent gas flow, H2S concentrations, and refinery -wide sulfur 
recovery, in the written follow -up report required by Condition 8077 -B9C(viii).  
(Basis: Recordkeeping, NSPS, Regulation 9- 1-313.2, Regulation 12- 11-401)  
 
The complete condition that will be issued as a part of the  permit to oper ate is stated in the following section.

blocks.heading: 1. Background
blocks.text:
1. Background
On September 7, 2018, an Authority to Construct was issued for the installation of the below equipment located 
at Tesoro Refining and Manufacturing Company LLC (Tesoro) : 
S-1517  Coker Flare  
Abated by  
A-1583  Flare Gas H 2S Treatment System, Caustic Scrubbers (2)  & Knockout Drum , VaporLock  
This addendum serves to capture subsequent  changes to the conditions  after issuance  of the Authority to 
Construct. These revisions were made  in response to Tesoro’s comments concerning the monitoring of sulfur 
remov ed by A -1583.  
For reference, Tesoro’s comments are summarized below: 
• Concerning Part 69g –  This condition is unnecessary as there is no specific target for how much the H 2S 
treatment is required to remove.  
• Concerning Part 70 –  This condition is the same a s Part 69 and is redundant with respect to Condition 8077 
requirements.  
 
As noted in the engineering eval uation, section 5, t hese comments were consider ed by the District, however, 
upon  initial assessment , they were  deemed unwarranted without additional  information concerning  Tesoro ’s 
fulfillment of the  regulatory sulfur recovery requirements stated in  Regulation 9 -1-313.2.  Therefore, the District 
did not implement any revisions. D ue to the tight timeline in which Tesoro needed to install  A-1583  before the 
turnaround, Tesoro accepted issuance of the Authority to Construct with Condition 23129 “as -is”, contingent 
upon the further review and amendment of the referenced per mit conditions by the District before issuance of 
the final permit to operat e. 
The District requested that Tesoro submit additional information concerning the design  parameters  of A-1583. 
In response, Tesoro submitted information showing that , subject to a fixed inlet concentration of H 2S, greater 
than 95%  sulfur removal can be ac hieved by A -1583 (see Attachment A).  The following section summarizes the 
District’s review of this information and subsequent revisions  to the referenced condition parts.  
2. District’s  Review & Revision   
Part 69 g.  
As seen from the  information provided by Tesoro,  A-1583 is designed to output  a stream with a maximum H2S 
concentration of 80 ppmv. This design allows  A-1583 to achieve  95% sulfur recovery  only if the inlet  
concentr ation of H 2S remains above 3240 ppmv . Therefore , to ensure a sulfur recovery  of at least 95 %, the 
District would have to limit the inlet H 2S concentration to above  a certain amount. This type of limitation could 
be detrimental to the  overall goal of sulfur reduction.  
In addition, as stated in Regulation 9 -1-313. 2, the 95% recovery applies on a refinery-wide basis and is not 
meant to apply to a single piece of equipment. For the reasons stated, the District has amended the conditions 
to allow for either measurement of sulfur recovered by A-1583 or total exclusion of the sulfur recovered by       
2 
 A-1583 when performing a refinery -wide balance to determine sulfur recovery . This is summarized in the revised 
part 69 g below:  
69 g . The owner/operator shall choose one of the following recordkeeping/evaluation methods for determination of 
sulfur recovery on a refinery -wide basis during the operation of A -1583:  
i.  Record amount of sulfur removed from the vent gas in A -1583 OR 
ii. Exclude amount of sulfur removed from the vent gas in A -1583 from  the calculation of total sulfur 
recovered on a refinery- wide basis.   
 
Part 70 
To address Tesoro’s issue of redundancy from the implementation of both Condition 8077 and Condition 23129, 
Part 70, this condition  part was revised to  reference 8077 only if t he operation of A-1583 is being u sed in 
replacement of a measure defined under 8077 -B9C (i -vi). These revisions  are summarized  as follows:  
70. If the owner/operator is using the operation of A- 1583 in lieu of any measure set forth per 
Condition 8077 -B9C (i -vi) then the owner/operator shall include a summary of the A- 1583 operation 
during the turnaround, including vent gas flow, H2S concentrations, and refinery -wide sulfur 
recovery, in the written follow -up report required by Condition 8077 -B9C(viii).  
(Basis: Recordkeeping, NSPS, Regulation 9- 1-313.2, Regulation 12- 11-401)  
 
The complete condition that will be issued as a part of the  permit to oper ate is stated in the following section.

unmapped_blocks.text:
EVALUATION REPORT
Tesoro Refining and Manufacturing Company LLC  
150 Solano  Way , Martinez, CA 94553  
Plant # 14628, Application # 29416 
1. Background  
On September 7, 2018, an Authority to Construct was issued for the installation of the below equipment located 
at Tesoro Refining and Manufacturing Company LLC (Tesoro) : 
S-1517  Coker Flare  
Abated by  
A-1583  Flare Gas H 2S Treatment System, Caustic Scrubbers (2)  & Knockout Drum , VaporLock  
This addendum serves to capture subsequent  changes to the conditions  after issuance  of the Authority to 
Construct. These revisions were made  in response to Tesoro’s comments concerning the monitoring of sulfur 
remov ed by A -1583.  
For reference, Tesoro’s comments are summarized below: 
• Concerning Part 69g –  This condition is unnecessary as there is no specific target for how much the H 2S 
treatment is required to remove.  
• Concerning Part 70 –  This condition is the same a s Part 69 and is redundant with respect to Condition 8077 
requirements.  
 
As noted in the engineering eval uation, section 5, t hese comments were consider ed by the District, however, 
upon  initial assessment , they were  deemed unwarranted without additional  information concerning  Tesoro ’s 
fulfillment of the  regulatory sulfur recovery requirements stated in  Regulation 9 -1-313.2.  Therefore, the District 
did not implement any revisions. D ue to the tight timeline in which Tesoro needed to install  A-1583  before the 
turnaround, Tesoro accepted issuance of the Authority to Construct with Condition 23129 “as -is”, contingent 
upon the further review and amendment of the referenced per mit conditions by the District before issuance of 
the final permit to operat e. 
The District requested that Tesoro submit additional information concerning the design  parameters  of A-1583. 
In response, Tesoro submitted information showing that , subject to a fixed inlet concentration of H 2S, greater 
than 95%  sulfur removal can be ac hieved by A -1583 (see Attachment A).  The following section summarizes the 
District’s review of this information and subsequent revisions  to the referenced condition parts.  
2. District’s  Review & Revision   
Part 69 g.  
As seen from the  information provided by Tesoro,  A-1583 is designed to output  a stream with a maximum H2S 
concentration of 80 ppmv. This design allows  A-1583 to achieve  95% sulfur recovery  only if the inlet  
concentr ation of H 2S remains above 3240 ppmv . Therefore , to ensure a sulfur recovery  of at least 95 %, the 
District would have to limit the inlet H 2S concentration to above  a certain amount. This type of limitation could 
be detrimental to the  overall goal of sulfur reduction.  
In addition, as stated in Regulation 9 -1-313. 2, the 95% recovery applies on a refinery-wide basis and is not 
meant to apply to a single piece of equipment. For the reasons stated, the District has amended the conditions 
to allow for either measurement of sulfur recovered by A-1583 or total exclusion of the sulfur recovered by       
2 
 A-1583 when performing a refinery -wide balance to determine sulfur recovery . This is summarized in the revised 
part 69 g below:  
69 g . The owner/operator shall choose one of the following recordkeeping/evaluation methods for determination of 
sulfur recovery on a refinery -wide basis during the operation of A -1583:  
i.  Record amount of sulfur removed from the vent gas in A -1583 OR 
ii. Exclude amount of sulfur removed from the vent gas in A -1583 from  the calculation of total sulfur 
recovered on a refinery- wide basis.   
 
Part 70 
To address Tesoro’s issue of redundancy from the implementation of both Condition 8077 and Condition 23129, 
Part 70, this condition  part was revised to  reference 8077 only if t he operation of A-1583 is being u sed in 
replacement of a measure defined under 8077 -B9C (i -vi). These revisions  are summarized  as follows:  
70. If the owner/operator is using the operation of A- 1583 in lieu of any measure set forth per 
Condition 8077 -B9C (i -vi) then the owner/operator shall include a summary of the A- 1583 operation 
during the turnaround, including vent gas flow, H2S concentrations, and refinery -wide sulfur 
recovery, in the written follow -up report required by Condition 8077 -B9C(viii).  
(Basis: Recordkeeping, NSPS, Regulation 9- 1-313.2, Regulation 12- 11-401)  
 
The complete condition that will be issued as a part of the  permit to oper ate is stated in the following section.  
 
 
3. Conditions  
Condition # 23129

     18311 (Modify Part 26 -  Initial source tests for

     through 68 for S- 1517.

     ensure that the proposed project complies with all 
     applicable District, State, and Federal

      Regulations. The conditions limit operational  
     parameters such as fuel use, stack gas emission  
     concentrations, and mass emission rates. Permit  
     conditions will also specify abatement device 
     operation and performance levels. For compliance  
     assurance purpose, conditions specifying emission  
     monitoring, source testing, and record keeping  
     requirements are included. Furthermore, pollutant  
     mass emission limits (in uni ts of lb./hr) will  
     ensure that daily and annual emission rate  
     limitations are not exceeded.                               
 
     Compliance with CO and NOx limitations will be  
     verified by continuous in- stack emission monitors  
     (CEMs) that will be in operation during all heater  
     operating modes, including start up and shutdown.  
     Compliance with SO2 and H2S limits will be  
     determined by monitoring the total reduced sulfur  
     (TRS) concentration level in the refinery fuel gas  
     with a TRS analyzer. If natural gas is burned, the  
     sulfur content will be assumed to be the same as  
     natural gas specifications.  Compliance with POC  
     and PM10 mass emission limits will be demonstrated  
     by annual source testing.
